args:
cat > /workspace/compute-source-env.sh << 'EIGEN_FILE_EOF'
#!/bin/sh
# EigenCompute container entrypoint script
# This script handles KMS secret fetching, TLS setup, and privilege dropping
# before executing the user's application.
#
# Template variables (replaced at build time):
#   KMS_SERVER_URL - URL of the KMS server
#   USER_API_URL - URL of the user API
#   KMS_SIGNING_PUBLIC_KEY - PEM-encoded KMS signing public key (embedded in heredoc below)

echo "compute-source-env.sh: Running setup script..."

# Write embedded KMS signing key to temp file
KMS_KEY_FILE=$(mktemp)
cat > "$KMS_KEY_FILE" << 'EIGEN_KMS_KEY_EOF'
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
MFkwEwYHKoZIzj0CAQYIKoZIzj0DAQcDQgAEb2Q88/cxdic2xi4jS2V0dtYHjLwq
4wVFBFmaY8TTXoMXNggKEdU6PuE8EovocVKMpw3SIlaM27z9uxksNVL2xw==
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

EIGEN_KMS_KEY_EOF

# Log the KMS signing public key for transparency
echo "=============================================="
echo "KMS Signing Public Key (used to verify secrets):"
echo "=============================================="
cat "$KMS_KEY_FILE"
echo "=============================================="

# Fetch and source environment variables from KMS
echo "Fetching secrets from KMS..."
if SSL_CERT_FILE=/usr/local/share/eigenx-ca-certs.crt /usr/local/bin/kms-client \
  --kms-server-url "http://10.128.0.57:8080" \
  --kms-signing-key-file "$KMS_KEY_FILE" \
  --userapi-url "https://userapi-compute-sepolia-dev.eigencloud.xyz" \
  --output /tmp/.env; then
    echo "compute-source-env.sh: Successfully fetched environment variables from KMS"
    set -a && . /tmp/.env && set +a
    rm -f /tmp/.env
else
    echo "compute-source-env.sh: ERROR - Failed to fetch environment variables from KMS"
    echo "compute-source-env.sh: Exiting - cannot start user workload without KMS secrets"
    rm -f "$KMS_KEY_FILE"
    exit 1
fi

# Clean up key file
rm -f "$KMS_KEY_FILE"

# Setup TLS if tls-client is present and DOMAIN is configured
setup_tls() {
    # If tls-client isn't present, TLS wasn't configured during build
    if [ ! -x /usr/local/bin/tls-client ]; then
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: TLS not configured (no tls-client binary)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local domain="${DOMAIN:-}"
    local mnemonic="${MNEMONIC:-}"
    
    # If DOMAIN is not set or is localhost, skip TLS setup
    if [ -z "$domain" ] || [ "$domain" = "localhost" ]; then
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: TLS skipped (DOMAIN not set or is localhost)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    if [ -z "$mnemonic" ]; then
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: ERROR - TLS requested but MNEMONIC not available"
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: Cannot obtain TLS certificate without mnemonic"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    if [ ! -x /usr/local/bin/caddy ]; then
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: ERROR - TLS requested but Caddy not found"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    echo "compute-source-env.sh: Setting up TLS for domain: $domain"
    
    # Obtain TLS certificate using ACME
    local challenge="${ACME_CHALLENGE:-http-01}"
    
    # Check if we should use staging (for testing)
    local staging_flag=""
    if [ "${ACME_STAGING:-false}" = "true" ]; then
        staging_flag="-staging"
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: Using Let's Encrypt STAGING environment"
    fi
    
    echo "compute-source-env.sh: Obtaining TLS certificate using $challenge challenge..."
    if ! SSL_CERT_FILE=/usr/local/share/eigenx-ca-certs.crt \
       MNEMONIC="$mnemonic" DOMAIN="$domain" API_URL="https://userapi-compute-sepolia-dev.eigencloud.xyz" /usr/local/bin/tls-client \
        -challenge "$challenge" \
        $staging_flag; then
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: ERROR - Failed to obtain TLS certificate"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    echo "compute-source-env.sh: TLS certificate obtained successfully"
    
    # Validate Caddyfile before starting
    if [ -f /etc/caddy/Caddyfile ]; then
        if ! /usr/local/bin/caddy validate --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile --adapter caddyfile 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "compute-source-env.sh: ERROR - Invalid Caddyfile"
            exit 1
        fi
        
        # Start Caddy in background
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: Starting Caddy reverse proxy..."
        if [ "${ENABLE_CADDY_LOGS:-false}" = "true" ]; then
            if ! /usr/local/bin/caddy start --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile --adapter caddyfile 2>&1; then
                echo "compute-source-env.sh: ERROR - Failed to start Caddy"
                exit 1
            fi
        else
            if ! /usr/local/bin/caddy start --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile --adapter caddyfile >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                echo "compute-source-env.sh: ERROR - Failed to start Caddy"
                exit 1
            fi
        fi
        
        sleep 2
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: Caddy started successfully"
    else
        echo "compute-source-env.sh: No Caddyfile found, skipping Caddy"
    fi
    
    return 0
}

# Run TLS setup
setup_tls

# Export KMS variables for attestation-based JWT auth
export KMS_SERVER_URL="http://10.128.0.57:8080"
export KMS_PUBLIC_KEY="$(cat << 'EIGEN_KMS_PUB_EOF'
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
MFkwEwYHKoZIzj0CAQYIKoZIzj0DAQcDQgAEb2Q88/cxdic2xi4jS2V0dtYHjLwq
4wVFBFmaY8TTXoMXNggKEdU6PuE8EovocVKMpw3SIlaM27z9uxksNVL2xw==
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

EIGEN_KMS_PUB_EOF
)"

echo "compute-source-env.sh: Environment sourced."

# Drop privileges to original user for the application command
if [ -n "$__ECLOUD_ORIGINAL_USER" ] && [ "$(id -u)" = "0" ]; then
    echo "compute-source-env.sh: Dropping privileges to user: $__ECLOUD_ORIGINAL_USER"
    exec su -s /bin/sh "$__ECLOUD_ORIGINAL_USER" -c 'exec "$@"' -- sh "$@"
fi

exec "$@"

EIGEN_FILE_EOF
steps.name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker

args:
#!/bin/bash
# Extract binaries from dependency images
# Binaries are extracted from /eigen/bin/ to /workspace/deps/
#
# Template variables (replaced at build time):
#   echo "Extracting kms-client..."
cid=$(docker create docker.io/eigenlayer/eigencloud-containers@sha256:c192a771ee8c72d95168ca4b524956a4b730c3f7b6a73b5533e4b8a43ad72fe4)
docker cp "$cid:/eigen/bin/kms-client" /workspace/deps/kms-client
docker rm "$cid" >/dev/null

echo "Extracting tls-client..."
cid=$(docker create docker.io/eigenlayer/eigencloud-containers@sha256:e3e82a690f61c621fb511e9b3e33fdb6e290d8f1efde6938b0eae5628a6c338c)
docker cp "$cid:/eigen/bin/tls-client" /workspace/deps/tls-client
docker rm "$cid" >/dev/null - Generated extraction commands for each dep

set -ex  # -e: exit on error, -x: print each command before executing

echo "=== Starting dependency extraction ==="
mkdir -p /workspace/deps
echo "Created /workspace/deps directory"

echo "Extracting kms-client..."
cid=$(docker create docker.io/eigenlayer/eigencloud-containers@sha256:c192a771ee8c72d95168ca4b524956a4b730c3f7b6a73b5533e4b8a43ad72fe4)
docker cp "$cid:/eigen/bin/kms-client" /workspace/deps/kms-client
docker rm "$cid" >/dev/null

echo "Extracting tls-client..."
cid=$(docker create docker.io/eigenlayer/eigencloud-containers@sha256:e3e82a690f61c621fb511e9b3e33fdb6e290d8f1efde6938b0eae5628a6c338c)
docker cp "$cid:/eigen/bin/tls-client" /workspace/deps/tls-client
docker rm "$cid" >/dev/null

# Make all extracted binaries executable
if ls /workspace/deps/* 1>/dev/null 2>&1; then
    chmod +x /workspace/deps/*
    echo "Dependency binaries extracted:"
    ls -la /workspace/deps/
else
    echo "Warning: No dependency binaries found in /workspace/deps/"
fi
steps.name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker

args:
#!/bin/bash
# Build the final layered TEE image with preserved CMD from user's image
set -e

# Extract original CMD from user's image
USER_CMD=$(docker inspect --format='{{json .Config.Cmd}}' us-east1-docker.pkg.dev/tee-compute-sepolia-dev/eigencloud-containers/2d1b4a59148274dd1e42e9540a94c81089f91cee:2d1b4a59148274dd1e42e9540a94c81089f91cee-1783547880-user)
echo "Original CMD from user image: $USER_CMD"

# Write the base Dockerfile
cat > /workspace/Dockerfile.layered << 'EIGEN_DOCKERFILE_EOF'
# Layered TEE Container Image
# Auto-generated Dockerfile that layers TEE infrastructure into user container
#
# Requirements: Base image must include /bin/sh

# Get Caddy from official image
FROM caddy:2.10.2-alpine AS caddy

FROM us-east1-docker.pkg.dev/tee-compute-sepolia-dev/eigencloud-containers/2d1b4a59148274dd1e42e9540a94c81089f91cee:2d1b4a59148274dd1e42e9540a94c81089f91cee-1783547880-user

# CA bundle for kms-client / tls-client to validate HTTPS calls to
# eigencloud.xyz endpoints. Bundled at a non-standard path and consumed
# only via SSL_CERT_FILE in compute-source-env.sh, so the user's
# /etc/ssl/ is never touched.
COPY --from=alpine:3.20.10 /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt /usr/local/share/eigenx-ca-certs.crt

# Copy entrypoint script (contains embedded KMS signing key)
COPY compute-source-env.sh /usr/local/bin/

# Copy Caddy from official image
COPY --from=caddy /usr/bin/caddy /usr/local/bin/caddy

# Copy all dependency binaries (kms-client, tls-client, user deps)
# Extracted from dependency images to /workspace/deps/ by build steps
COPY deps/ /usr/local/bin/
COPY Caddyfile /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
# Make all binaries executable
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/*

# TEE launch policy labels
LABEL tee.launch_policy.log_redirect=always
LABEL tee.launch_policy.monitoring_memory_allow=always
LABEL eigenx_vm_image=eigen

# Expose HTTP and HTTPS ports for Caddy
EXPOSE 80 443

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/compute-source-env.sh"]

EIGEN_DOCKERFILE_EOF

# Append CMD instruction if user image had one (not null or empty)
if [ "$USER_CMD" != "null" ] && [ -n "$USER_CMD" ] && [ "$USER_CMD" != "[]" ]; then
    echo '' >> /workspace/Dockerfile.layered
    echo '# Preserved from original user image' >> /workspace/Dockerfile.layered
    echo "CMD $USER_CMD" >> /workspace/Dockerfile.layered
    echo "Preserved CMD: $USER_CMD"
else
    echo 'No CMD found in user image (will use ENTRYPOINT only)'
fi

echo '=== Final Dockerfile.layered ==='
cat /workspace/Dockerfile.layered
echo '================================'

docker build -f /workspace/Dockerfile.layered -t us-east1-docker.pkg.dev/tee-compute-sepolia-dev/eigencloud-containers/2d1b4a59148274dd1e42e9540a94c81089f91cee:2d1b4a59148274dd1e42e9540a94c81089f91cee-1783547880 /workspace
steps.name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker

args:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Docker Hub authentication using Organization Access Token (OAT)
# Requires: DOCKERHUB_USERNAME (env var), DOCKERHUB_TOKEN (secret)
#
# NOTE: This script runs inside Cloud Build. Shell variables must be
# escaped with $ to prevent Cloud Build from treating them as substitutions.

set -euo pipefail

echo "Starting Docker Hub authentication..."

if [ -z "$DOCKERHUB_USERNAME" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: DOCKERHUB_USERNAME is not set"
  exit 1
fi

if [ -z "$DOCKERHUB_TOKEN" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: DOCKERHUB_TOKEN is not set"
  exit 1
fi

echo "Logging into Docker Hub as $DOCKERHUB_USERNAME..."
echo "$DOCKERHUB_TOKEN" | docker login -u "$DOCKERHUB_USERNAME" --password-stdin

echo "Docker Hub authentication successful"
steps.name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker

export KMS_PUBLIC_KEY="$(cat << 'EIGEN_KMS_PUB_EOF'
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
MFkwEwYHKoZIzj0CAQYIKoZIzj0DAQcDQgAEb2Q88/cxdic2xi4jS2V0dtYHjLwq
4wVFBFmaY8TTXoMXNggKEdU6PuE8EovocVKMpw3SIlaM27z9uxksNVL2xw==

EIGEN_KMS_PUB_EOF

if [ -n "$__ECLOUD_ORIGINAL_USER" ] && [ "$(id -u)" = "0" ]; then

args:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Docker Hub authentication using Organization Access Token (OAT)
# Requires: DOCKERHUB_USERNAME (env var), DOCKERHUB_TOKEN (secret)
#
# NOTE: This script runs inside Cloud Build. Shell variables must be
# escaped with $ to prevent Cloud Build from treating them as substitutions.

set -euo pipefail

echo "Starting Docker Hub authentication..."

if [ -z "$DOCKERHUB_USERNAME" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: DOCKERHUB_USERNAME is not set"
  exit 1
fi

if [ -z "$DOCKERHUB_TOKEN" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: DOCKERHUB_TOKEN is not set"
  exit 1
fi

echo "Logging into Docker Hub as $DOCKERHUB_USERNAME..."
echo "$DOCKERHUB_TOKEN" | docker login -u "$DOCKERHUB_USERNAME" --password-stdin

echo "Docker Hub authentication successful"
